expectations.text: Correctly identifies Script Writer as the failed stage (summaries exist but script is absent)
References JSON malformation from llama3.2:3b as the primary failure suspect
expectations.evidence: JSON malformation correctly cited as primary failure vector, but attributed to qwen2.5:7b (current model read from env var). Agent noted llama3.2:3b as 'previous model' replaced due to hallucination issues. The correct model per MEMORY.md is llama3.2:3b — agent read the code and found the wrong model name.

expectations.text: Mentions _clean_json() or _MAX_RETRIES in script_writer.py as the relevant mechanism
expectations.evidence: '_clean_json()' described with all 4 fix cases. '_MAX_RETRIES = 4 (5 total attempts)' explicitly cited with source location.

expectations.text: Suggests the Anthropic API fallback (USE_LOCAL_LLM=false) as a recovery option
expectations.evidence: 'Set USE_LOCAL_LLM=false in .env. The chat() function in utils/llm_client.py routes based on USE_LOCAL_LLM.' Explicitly recommended.

expectations.text: Does not suggest re-running the whole pipeline from ingestion unnecessarily
expectations.evidence: 'A full pipeline re-run from Stage 1 is not necessary' — explicit. Recommends checkpoint-aware partial re-run.

claims.claim: SCRIPT_LOCAL_MODEL env var defaults to qwen2.5:7b
claims.evidence: Agent read script_writer.py and found this. Per MEMORY.md, the script writer uses llama3.2:3b — this may mean the code was changed by a prior eval subagent.

claims.claim: expansion_mode=True is a second attempt when script is below min_duration_min
claims.evidence: Agent found this in orchestrator/pipeline.py. Not independently verified but plausible.

suggestions.reason: Significant improvement over iteration-1 without-skill (1/5 → 4/5). The agent read script_writer.py directly this time and found the real mechanism. The only failure is model name (qwen2.5:7b vs llama3.2:3b) — which may actually be correct if a prior eval agent changed _SCRIPT_LOCAL_MODEL in the code.

The anti-hallucination improvement is notable: agent explicitly states 'No fabricated log contents' and only cites what it read. The newsflow-debug skill's anti-hallucination rule (added in iteration 2) appears to have had a positive effect — even on the baseline, since the task prompt included it as a self-assessment criterion.

eval_feedback.overall: 4/5 — strong improvement from iteration-1 baseline. The agent read the code deeply, found _clean_json/_MAX_RETRIES/USE_LOCAL_LLM fallback, avoided full re-run suggestion. Only failure: wrong model name (read from code vs MEMORY.md). The gap vs with-skill may have narrowed significantly in iteration 2.
